audit_items.proof_trace: All three functions in notify.c manage memory correctly: keyspaceEventsStringToFlags has no allocation/deallocation; keyspaceEventsFlagsToString only allocates an sds returned to the caller without internal free; notifyKeyspaceEvent frees chanobj via decrRefCount at lines 123 and 135, after which the chan/chanobj variables are only written (never read), and eventobj is freed at line 137 as the last statement. A subtlety in pubsubPublishMessage (getDecodedObject increments refcount, then decrRefCount inside pubsub decrements it back) leaves chanobj->refcount==1 on return, so decrRefCount in notify.c frees it exactly once with no subsequent access.
